why_it_matters: Legato 1.1.0 adds verified cross-platform shared playback headers so applications can reuse static auth contexts across playlists without duplicating secrets on every track or leaking merged headers into public state.
user_impact: Consumers now have a coordinated 1.1.0 package/native line with `HeaderGroup`, `Track.headerGroupId`, Android+iOS runtime support, and smoke-validated mixed-token behavior.
upgrade_notes: Use @ddgutierrezc/legato-contract@1.1.0, @ddgutierrezc/legato-capacitor@1.1.0, dev.dgutierrez:legato-android-core:1.1.0, and LegatoCore v1.1.0. Resync Android/iOS hosts after upgrading the npm packages.
highlights: Shared playback headers are now part of the public contract and runtime package line.
Android and iOS resolve shared header groups with per-track override precedence and fail-fast validation.
Smoke/report validation now enforces shared group A/B, override, public-track non-leakage, and mixed-token playlist coverage.
known_limitations: Shared header groups remain static setup-time declarations; token refresh and DRM are still out of scope.
Advanced lifecycle/process-death hardening remains tracked independently from this feature release.
